channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: TANROADS completes 81 emergency bridges and culverts to reduce flood impact
item.description: The Minister for Works, Abdallah Ulega, has praised TANROADS for successfully completing 81 emergency infrastructure projects across Tanzania, highlighting the country’s improved preparedness against ...

item.title: Researchers Identify Brain Region That May Influence Hypertension and Suggest Treatment Approach
item.description: A recent study conducted by researchers from the University of São Paulo and the University of Auckland has identified a potential new cause of high blood pressure related to a specific brain region c...

item.title: Two children tragically die after being trapped in a car in Olongapo City
item.description: In Olongapo City, a tragic incident occurred where two young children lost their lives after being accidentally trapped inside a vehicle. Authorities reported that the children were found inside an ab...

item.title: Sudanese national charged in connection with fatalities during attempted Channel crossing
item.description: A Sudanese man has been formally charged in connection with the deaths of four people who lost their lives while attempting to cross the English Channel in an overloaded small boat. The tragic inciden...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Proposes Reforms to Filibuster Procedure
item.description: Democratic Maine Governor Janet Mills, who is campaigning to unseat long-serving Senator Susan Collins, has outlined a policy platform that includes reforming the Senate filibuster. Mills proposes a '...

item.title: Libya's rival legislative bodies approve first unified national budget in over a decade
item.description: In a significant political and economic development, Libya's two rival legislative bodies have approved the country's first unified state budget in more than ten years. According to a statement from t...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif holds talks with US Vice President JD Vance as US-Iran peace negotiations start in Islamabad
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met United States Vice President JD Vance in Islamabad on Saturday as high-level peace talks between the United States and Iran formally began. The Prime Minister describ...

item.title: First Nations challenge Alberta independence bid over treaty rights
item.description: In Alberta, several First Nations, including Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation and Athabasca Chipewyan First Nation, are challenging the province's potential separation from Canada, arguing it would violate c...

item.title: Ten Indian Nationals Indicted in the US for Visa Fraud via Staged Robberies
item.description: Ten Indian citizens have been formally indicted by a federal grand jury in Boston, United States, for allegedly conspiring to stage armed robberies at convenience stores and fast food restaurants. The...

item.title: JD Vance Leads US-Iran Peace Talks in Pakistan Amid Disputed Claims Over Frozen Assets
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance has arrived in Islamabad to lead high-stakes peace talks with Iran, following a temporary ceasefire after over a month of conflict. Iranian officials claimed the United Stat...

item.title: President Ruto Sets Up Selection Panels for Key Oversight and Education Offices
item.description: President William Ruto has constituted several selection panels to lead the recruitment of top officials for critical public institutions, including the Independent Policing Oversight Authority (IPOA)...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Accuses BJP of Trying to Cancel Her Bhabanipur Nomination and Deleting Names of 90 Lakh Voters
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister and Trinamool Congress supremo Mamata Banerjee addressed a massive election rally in Keshiyari, Paschim Medinipur district on April 11, 2026. In her fiery speech, she allege...

item.title: PIA plans long-term expansion to restore and grow its fleet
item.description: Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) is working towards reviving and expanding its operations with a strategic goal of building a 60-aircraft fleet, said Arif Habib, Chairman of the Arif Habib Consor...

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies Voting and Election Contestation Are Statutory Rights, Not Fundamental
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has clarified that the right to vote and the right to contest elections do not constitute fundamental rights, but are statutory entitlements governed by law. The bench of Ju...

item.title: DA opens 2026 Federal Congress as party sets course for new leadership
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) officially opened its 2026 Federal Congress at the Gallagher Convention Centre in Midrand, marking a defining moment for the party as it prepares to elect new federal lead...

item.title: Elizabeth Fry Society loses shelter space as lease extension is denied in Edmonton
item.description: A women’s shelter operated by the Elizabeth Fry Society in northeast Edmonton has been forced to shut down after the City of Edmonton refused to extend its lease. Originally intended as a temporary fa...

item.title: Political entrepreneurs exploiting state and criminal networks in South Africa
item.description: Half a year into the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry and following the ad hoc committee investigating Lt General Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi, it has become apparent that South Africa faces a distinct and danger...

item.title: JD Vance Visits Islamabad Amid US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: The 50th US Vice President, JD Vance, arrived in Islamabad on April 11, 2026, marking the first US vice-presidential visit to Pakistan since Joe Biden in 2011. Vance's visit coincides with Pakistan-br...

item.title: ACT night-time businesses struggle under regulatory and cost pressures
item.description: Owners of nightclubs and live music venues in Canberra have raised concerns that the territory's regulations and high costs are stifling the city's night-time economy. Submissions to a Legislative Ass...

item.title: Hungary Faces Critical Election Amid Orbán's Controversial Leadership
item.description: Hungary is heading to a pivotal election this weekend, with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, in power since 2010, potentially facing removal. Orbán, a conservative nationalist praised by both Donald Trump...
